title: AI Fundamentals
description: The vocabulary and mental models you need to follow AI conversations, ask better questions, and tell signal from noise.

title: Transformers and Attention
description: How modern AI models understand text: from attention mechanics to the latest SSA architectures.

title: What is AI?
description: From rule-based systems to neural networks: what AI actually is, what it is not, and how the main branches relate to each other.

title: How LLMs Work
description: Tokens, embeddings, attention, and why models hallucinate. A practical look at what happens inside a language model.

title: How Models Are Trained
description: Pre-training, fine-tuning, RLHF, and alignment: where a model's behavior comes from and why it can be changed.

title: Context, Memory and Limits
description: What a context window is, why it matters, and how RAG and vector search extend what a model can work with.
